items.description: A temporal property assertion (safety, liveness, fairness)

fields.description: Formal expression defining the temporal property
fields.name: expression

fields.description: Category of temporal property (safety, liveness, or fairness)
fields.name: property_type

fields.description: Applicability scope of this property
fields.name: scope

fields.description: Free-form description of the property
fields.name: description

fields.description: External references (URLs, documents, issue links)
fields.name: refs

items.inference_guide: Look for system-wide temporal guarantees that must hold across all executions. Signals: safety properties ('X never happens'), liveness properties ('Y eventually happens'), fairness properties ('if X is requested, it is eventually granted'); invariants that span multiple behaviors; global ordering guarantees; eventual consistency promises; progress guarantees. Set property_type to 'safety', 'liveness', or 'fairness'. The expression field should state the property formally or semi-formally. Skip: local function postconditions (those are invariants), single-behavior contracts.
items.name: Property

fields.description: Formal expression stating the axiom
fields.name: expression

fields.description: Invariants this axiom assumes as foundational truths
fields.name: assumes

fields.description: Rationale for why this axiom is assumed true
fields.name: justification

fields.description: Free-form description of the axiom
fields.name: description

fields.description: External references (URLs, documents, issue links)
fields.name: refs

items.inference_guide: Look for foundational assumptions the system relies on without proving them. Signals: comments stating 'we assume', 'given that', 'prerequisite'; environment assumptions (e.g., 'clock is monotonic', 'network is eventually connected'); trust boundaries ('upstream service guarantees X'); mathematical axioms in algorithm implementations; configuration assumptions that must hold for correctness. The expression field states what is assumed. Add justification explaining why the assumption is safe. Link assumes to invariants the axiom supports. Skip: derived properties (those are properties), things the system proves/enforces itself.
items.name: Axiom

items.description: A shared synchronization contract between processes

fields.description: Set of events that this protocol can communicate
fields.name: alphabet

fields.description: Possible states in the protocol state machine
fields.name: states

fields.description: State transitions triggered by events (from -> event -> to)
fields.name: transitions

fields.description: Starting state of the protocol
fields.name: initial_state

fields.description: Free-form description of the protocol
fields.name: description

fields.description: External references (URLs, documents, issue links)
fields.name: refs

items.inference_guide: Look for multi-party interaction patterns with defined message sequences and state machines. Signals: handshake sequences (connect/auth/ready); transaction protocols (begin/commit/rollback); consensus algorithms; leader election protocols; retry/backoff protocols with defined states; connection lifecycle state machines; API versioning/negotiation protocols. Define alphabet (set of events), states, transitions, and initial_state. One protocol per distinct coordination pattern shared between processes. Skip: single-party state machines (those are processes), simple request-response patterns, stateless interactions.
items.name: Protocol

items.description: A mapping from abstract to concrete specification

fields.description: Reference to the abstract behavior being refined
fields.name: abstract_entity

fields.description: Reference to the concrete behavior that implements the refinement
fields.name: concrete_entity

fields.description: Changes to invariants introduced or relaxed by this refinement
fields.name: invariant_deltas

fields.description: Next refinement in the refinement chain
fields.name: chains_to

fields.description: Free-form description of the refinement mapping
fields.name: description

fields.description: External references (URLs, documents, issue links)
fields.name: refs

items.inference_guide: Look for abstract-to-concrete implementation mappings where a high-level specification is progressively detailed. Signals: abstract base classes with concrete implementations; interface + implementation pairs where the implementation adds constraints; layered architectures where higher layers define contracts and lower layers fulfill them; strategy patterns; template method patterns. Link abstract_entity to the high-level behavior and concrete_entity to the implementation. List invariant_deltas for constraints added or relaxed. Chain refinements with chains_to for multi-step refinement. Skip: simple inheritance, polymorphism without specification changes.
items.name: Refinement

items.description: A CSP-style communicating process with states and transitions

fields.description: Set of events that this process can communicate
fields.name: alphabet

fields.description: Possible states in the process state machine
fields.name: states

fields.description: Starting state of the process
fields.name: initial_state

fields.description: Composition operator and sub-processes (e.g. parallel, sequential, choice)
fields.name: composition

fields.description: Sub-processes composed by this process
fields.name: sub_processes

fields.description: Free-form description of the process
fields.name: description

fields.description: External references (URLs, documents, issue links)
fields.name: refs

items.inference_guide: Look for long-running stateful components that communicate via events and have defined lifecycles. Signals: actor/agent implementations; background workers with state machines; sagas/orchestrators with defined steps; finite state machines in code; async task pipelines; microservice interaction patterns with defined communication channels. Define alphabet (events it sends/receives), states, initial_state, and composition (parallel, sequential, choice). Link sub_processes for composed processes. Skip: stateless request handlers (those are behaviors), simple event handlers, one-shot tasks.
items.name: Process
